payload.answer: These two statements conflict: if Sara is your daughter, she is not your niece. I would keep both as conflicting relationship claims and ask which relationship is correct (or whether the statements refer to different people named Sara).
payload.conflict_classification: Direct relationship conflict (same subject, different kinship relations: daughter vs niece).
payload.decision: Do not overwrite existing durable memory; store both relationship claims as conflicting/ambiguous unless clarification is provided.

entity_resolution.reason: Both inputs refer to the same named subject 'Sara' but with mutually inconsistent kinship roles (daughter vs niece). Keep as separate, conflicting claims pending clarification.

details: Flagged conflicting kinship relations for the same subject name 'Sara'.
Chose not to overwrite existing durable memory; preserved both claims as ambiguous/conflicting.

repair_options: Confirm whether Sara is your daughter, your niece, or a different person with the same name.
If Sara is your niece, identify whose sister/brother she is (e.g., 'Sara is the daughter of my sibling X').
If Sara is your daughter, confirm the parent-child link (e.g., 'Sara is my biological/adopted daughter').
Provide contextual metadata (approximate dates/age or location) to disambiguate which Sara the statements refer to.
